generic_rules: Focus repairs on resolving AGREE syntax errors and type mismatches.
Prioritize repairs that use visible AADL features or locally declared helpers.
Ensure AGREE expressions have valid types (int, real, bool).
Use concrete bounds or visible relations when defining range assumptions/guarantees.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts reside within `annex agree { ... };` blocks.
Range guarantees often follow the pattern: `eq range_name : bool = x >= lower_limit and x <= upper_limit;`
anti_patterns: Avoid introducing new architecture to fix AGREE errors; focus on contract adjustments.
Do not attempt to use implementation-level details in component type contracts.
Do not use undefined or invalid AGREE type names (e.g., 'integer').
irrelevant_topics: Component type scopes vs. implementation scopes
